nodes.label: CMOS-integrated Organic Neuromorphic Imagers for Dual-Modal Imaging
nodes.summary: A 640x512-pixel CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imager that operates in two modes: standard frame-based imaging and synaptic neuromorphic imaging. The synaptic mode extracts high-resolution spatiotemporal maps (light distribution and motion trajectories) from final frames by decoding temporal sequences of light events through contrast analysis. Adjustable memory (delay 10^1 to 10^4 ms) via modulation of charge recombination-trapping dynamics; retention >18 min. Demonstrates potential for industrial monitoring and motion detection.
nodes.source_file: raw/CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imagers for high-resolution dual-modal imaging.pdf

nodes.label: Hybrid CMOS + organic BHJ device stack
nodes.summary: Lateral two-terminal organic neuromorphic device with donor-acceptor bulk heterojunctions (BHJ) of PTB7-Th and PZ1 between Au electrodes, with (3-Mercaptopropyl)trimethoxysilane (MPTS) as an interfacial trap layer. Without MPTS, memory effects are negligible. The BHJ + trap stack is co-integrated with a readout integrated circuit (ROIC) via a CMOS-compatible photolithography method using photocrosslinking polymers, enabling non-destructive patterning down to ~1 um and monolithic integration of organic synaptic pixels on CMOS.
nodes.source_file: raw/CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imagers for high-resolution dual-modal imaging.pdf

nodes.label: Dual-modal operation: static frame + dynamic synaptic
nodes.summary: The imager operates in two modes. Standard mode: conventional frame-based static image capture via the ROIC. Synaptic mode: the organic device's photonic non-volatility (STP->LTP transition, paired-pulse facilitation, retention >18 min) accumulates light events so that a single final frame encodes the motion trajectory / light-event distribution map over tens of minutes. Dynamic information is preprocessed inside the pixel, eliminating the need to store/transmit every frame.
nodes.source_file: raw/CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imagers for high-resolution dual-modal imaging.pdf

nodes.label: Charge recombination-trapping dynamics as tunable memory mechanism
nodes.summary: The learning/memory mechanism is physical rather than algorithmic: photogenerated electrons trapped at the MPTS interface layer while holes flow, leading to a sublinear EPSC rise/decay. The decay time constant tau_0 (time to 1/e of peak EPSC) is tuned by the weight percentage of PZ1, which modifies the density of recombination centers. This gives adjustable delay from 10^1 to 10^4 ms, STP->LTP transitions, photonic writing and electrical erasing at -5 V, and energy of ~4 fJ per event at 0.2 V / 5 ms.
nodes.source_file: raw/CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imagers for high-resolution dual-modal imaging.pdf

nodes.label: CMOS-compatible photocrosslinking patterning for organic BHJs
nodes.summary: A photolithography method that uses photocrosslinking between polymers to pattern organic bulk heterojunctions non-destructively at ~1 um resolution, the key enabling step for integrating organic neuromorphic devices with wafer-scale CMOS readout at 640x512 resolution. Prior organic neuromorphic devices could not be patterned without damage, which had previously blocked high-resolution dual-modal imagers.
nodes.source_file: raw/CMOS-integrated organic neuromorphic imagers for high-resolution dual-modal imaging.pdf
